title: IELTS/TOEFL Score Converter 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Convert Between IELTS and TOEFL Scores
description: Convert IELTS, TOEFL iBT, and CEFR scores instantly. Free equivalency calculator with 2026 conversion tables and proficiency levels.
intro: This converter maps IELTS Band Scores (0–9, in 0.5 increments) to TOEFL iBT scores (0–120) and vice versa, then places both on the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR) scale (A1–C2). Neither ETS nor British Council publishes a single official conversion formula — instead, ETS released a concordance table in 2010 (updated 2023) based on linking studies comparing real test-taker performance. IELTS uses a 9-band holistic scale; TOEFL iBT is a four-skill test scored 0–120 (Reading 0–30, Listening 0–30, Speaking 0–30, Writing 0–30). Use this tool when applying to universities, immigration programs, or jobs that accept either exam, so you can compare your score to the institution's minimum requirement on the other scale.
keyTakeaway: **IELTS 7.0 ≈ TOEFL iBT 94–101 ≈ CEFR C1** — a score accepted by most top-tier English-medium universities worldwide (e.g., U.S. universities typically require TOEFL iBT ≥80 or IELTS ≥6.5).
useCases: A student with a TOEFL iBT score of 100 wants to apply to a UK university that lists IELTS 7.0 as its minimum requirement — the converter confirms equivalency and eligibility.
A Canadian permanent residency applicant (Express Entry) must meet CLB 7; they hold an IELTS 6.0 and need to know the TOEFL iBT equivalent to decide which retake is more strategic.
An employee relocating to Australia under a skilled-worker visa needs IELTS 7.0 (or equivalent) and wants to submit an existing TOEFL iBT 105 score instead.
A graduate program admissions officer is reviewing a mix of IELTS and TOEFL applications and needs a consistent CEFR level to rank candidates on the same proficiency scale.
A test-prep instructor advises students on whether their current IELTS 5.5 practice score corresponds to a TOEFL iBT band and what improvement is needed to reach C1.
An HR department sets a language requirement of C1 (CEFR) for a multinational company and needs to communicate equivalent cutoffs for both IELTS and TOEFL applicants.
explanation:
## How It's Calculated

There is no algebraic formula to convert IELTS to TOEFL — the conversion is **table-based**, derived from empirical linking studies. ETS (makers of TOEFL) and Cambridge/IDP (makers of IELTS) each conducted separate research; the most widely cited mapping is the ETS Concordance Table (2010, republished 2023):

```
IELTS Band  →  TOEFL iBT range  →  CEFR Level
   9.0             116–120              C2
   8.5             110–115              C2
   8.0             102–109              C2
   7.5              94–101              C1
   7.0              94–101              C1
   6.5              79–93               B2
   6.0              60–78               B2
   5.5              46–59               B1/B2
   5.0              35–45               B1
   4.5              32–34               B1
   4.0              --                  A2/B1
```

The converter applies the mid-point of each TOEFL range when a single number is needed:
```
TOEFL_mid = floor((TOEFL_low + TOEFL_high) / 2)
Example: IELTS 7.0 → TOEFL range 94–101 → mid = floor((94+101)/2) = 97
```

CEFR levels are assigned using the Cambridge English – CEFR alignment:
```
C2  → IELTS 8.5–9.0  / TOEFL iBT 110–120
C1  → IELTS 7.0–8.0  / TOEFL iBT 94–109  (some tables: 95–101 for 7.0)
B2  → IELTS 5.5–6.5  / TOEFL iBT 72–94
B1  → IELTS 4.0–5.0  / TOEFL iBT 42–71
A2  → IELTS <4.0     / TOEFL iBT <42
```

---

## Reference Table

| IELTS Band | TOEFL iBT (range) | TOEFL iBT (approx. mid) | CEFR | Proficiency Label |
|:----------:|:-----------------:|:-----------------------:|:----:|:-----------------:|
| 9.0        | 118–120           | 119                     | C2   | Mastery           |
| 8.5        | 110–117           | 114                     | C2   | Mastery           |
| 8.0        | 102–109           | 106                     | C2   | Advanced+         |
| 7.5        | 94–101            | 98                      | C1   | Advanced          |
| 7.0        | 94–101            | 97                      | C1   | Advanced          |
| 6.5        | 79–93             | 86                      | B2   | Upper-Intermediate|
| 6.0        | 60–78             | 69                      | B2   | Upper-Intermediate|
| 5.5        | 46–59             | 53                      | B1+  | Intermediate      |
| 5.0        | 35–45             | 40                      | B1   | Intermediate      |
| 4.5        | 32–34             | 33                      | B1   | Elementary+       |

*Source: ETS TOEFL/IELTS Concordance Table; Cambridge English CEFR scale.*

---

## Typical Cases

**Case 1 — University admission (US master's program):**
A student scored IELTS 7.0. Most U.S. research universities require TOEFL iBT ≥80–100. IELTS 7.0 maps to TOEFL iBT 94–101, so the student **exceeds** the 80-point floor and meets the 100-point threshold at the top of the range. CEFR: C1 — universally accepted for graduate study.

**Case 2 — UK Tier 4 Student Visa:**
UK Visas and Immigration (UKVI) requires IELTS Academic ≥6.0 (CEFR B2) for degree-level entry. A student holding TOEFL iBT 72 converts to approximately IELTS 6.0–6.5, placing them right at the B2 boundary. They should verify whether their specific institution accepts TOEFL for UKVI purposes (not all do).

**Case 3 — Canadian Express Entry (CLB 7):**
CLB 7 corresponds to IELTS 6.0 overall (with no band below 6.0) or TOEFL iBT total ≥83. A candidate with TOEFL iBT 88 maps to IELTS 6.5 — satisfying CLB 8 and earning **50 CRS points** for language proficiency versus 46 at CLB 7.

---

## Common Errors

1. **Treating the conversion as exact.** IELTS 7.0 and TOEFL iBT 94 are *statistically comparable*, not mathematically equal. Admissions offices may weight them differently.

2. **Ignoring sub-score requirements.** Many programs require minimum scores *per skill* (e.g., Writing ≥24/30 on TOEFL iBT). An overall band match does not guarantee sub-skill eligibility.

3. **Using outdated concordance tables.** ETS revised guidance in 2023 after TOEFL iBT was restructured (Reading and Listening sections shortened). Older tables may over- or under-estimate equivalencies by 2–5 points.

4. **Confusing IELTS Academic vs. IELTS General Training.** Immigration programs (e.g., Canada, Australia) often accept IELTS General Training; universities require IELTS Academic. The band-to-CEFR mapping is the same, but the tests differ in difficulty and acceptability.

5. **Rounding IELTS bands incorrectly.** IELTS reports in 0.5 increments (5.0, 5.5, 6.0…). A raw average of 6.25 rounds to 6.5 on the official IELTS scale — using 6.25 as input will produce a misleading conversion.

6. **Assuming CEFR C1 is sufficient everywhere.** Some elite programs (e.g., Oxford, Cambridge) require IELTS 7.5–8.0 per component, which sits above the C1/C2 boundary — a generic "C1 = accepted" rule fails here.

---

## Related Calculators

Since no related slugs were provided, explore other education tools on the site to complement your language-test planning.

faq.q: Is there an official IELTS-to-TOEFL conversion formula?
faq.a: No algebraic formula exists. ETS published a concordance table based on a statistical linking study comparing scores of test-takers who sat both exams. The table gives *ranges*, not exact equivalents — for example, IELTS 7.0 corresponds to TOEFL iBT 94–101 (a span of 8 points). Always check the institution's own equivalency policy, as some universities publish their own mapping.

faq.q: What TOEFL iBT score equals IELTS 6.5?
faq.a: According to the ETS concordance table, IELTS 6.5 maps to TOEFL iBT 79–93, with a midpoint of approximately 86. This band corresponds to CEFR B2 (Upper-Intermediate). Most U.S. universities set their minimum English-proficiency requirement at TOEFL iBT 80 (≈ IELTS 6.5), making this a common threshold for graduate admission.

faq.q: Which exam is easier to score high on — IELTS or TOEFL?
faq.a: Neither is objectively 'easier'; difficulty is test-taker dependent. IELTS uses British/Australian/North American accents and a paper-based Writing section; TOEFL iBT is fully computer-based with an integrated Academic English style. Research indicates that test-takers who are stronger at academic writing may prefer TOEFL iBT, while those comfortable with conversational listening may find IELTS Speaking (face-to-face) less stressful.

faq.q: Do US universities accept IELTS in place of TOEFL?
faq.a: Yes — as of 2024, virtually all accredited U.S. universities accept both IELTS Academic and TOEFL iBT. A common cutoff is TOEFL iBT ≥80 or IELTS ≥6.5 for undergraduate admission, and TOEFL iBT ≥100 or IELTS ≥7.0 for competitive graduate programs (e.g., MIT, Stanford, Harvard). Always verify each program's specific requirement, as sub-score minimums vary.

faq.q: What CEFR level corresponds to IELTS 7.0?
faq.a: IELTS 7.0 is aligned to CEFR C1 (Advanced) by Cambridge English and the Association of Language Testers in Europe (ALTE). C1 means a user can express ideas fluently and spontaneously, use language flexibly for social, academic, and professional purposes. IELTS 8.0–9.0 corresponds to C2 (Mastery/Proficiency).

faq.q: How does the TOEFL iBT restructuring in 2023 affect score conversions?
faq.a: In August 2023, ETS shortened the TOEFL iBT: Reading dropped from 3–4 passages to 2 (20 questions, 35 min) and Listening dropped from 4–6 lectures to 3 (28 questions, 36 min). The maximum score remains 120. ETS updated its concordance guidance to reflect the revised test structure; concordance tables from before July 2023 may be slightly off. The 2023 ETS Score Comparison Tool is the authoritative reference.

faq.q: Can I use TOEFL iBT scores for Australian or Canadian immigration?
faq.a: For Canada (Express Entry), IRCC accepts TOEFL iBT under the Canadian Language Benchmarks (CLB) system — TOEFL iBT ≥83 total (with minimums per skill) maps to CLB 7. Australia (General Skilled Migration) historically required IELTS, but since 2014 also accepts PTE Academic and, in some visa subclasses, TOEFL iBT. Check the specific visa subclass on the official DIBP website, as not all subclasses accept TOEFL.

faq.q: What is the minimum TOEFL iBT score that equals IELTS 5.0?
faq.a: IELTS 5.0 corresponds to TOEFL iBT 35–45 on the ETS concordance table (midpoint ≈ 40). This is CEFR B1 level — a user can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling and can produce simple connected text. Most degree-granting universities require scores well above this; B1 is more relevant for pathway/foundation programs or language-school enrollment.

faq.q: Are IELTS and TOEFL scores valid forever?
faq.a: No. Both exams have a standard validity period of **2 years** from the test date. Most universities, immigration authorities (IRCC, Home Office, DIBP), and professional licensing bodies will not accept scores older than 24 months at the time of application. Some institutions impose stricter 1-year windows for conditional offers. Always check the application deadline against your test date.
